input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

<current_datetime>2026-05-05T01:00:29.636Z</current_datetime>

I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
Here's how to make your MAUI page theme-aware:

## XAML — `AppThemeBinding`

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="16">
        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#E0E0E0}" />

        <Label Text="This page adapts to your system theme."
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#333333, Dark=#AAAAAA}" />

        <Button Text="Get Started"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

`AppThemeBinding` watches the OS theme and swaps values automatically — no event handling needed.

## C# alternative — `SetAppTheme`

For code-behind or dynamic scenarios, use `SetAppThemeColor` (for colors) or the general `SetAppTheme<T>`:

```csharp
// Colors
myLabel.SetAppThemeColor(Label.TextColorProperty,
    light: Colors.Black,
    dark:  Color.FromArgb("#E0E0E0"));

page.SetAppThemeColor(ContentPage.BackgroundColorProperty,
    light: Colors.White,
    dark:  Color.FromArgb("#1E1E1E"));

// Non-color properties (e.g. images, strings)
myImage.SetAppTheme<string>(Image.SourceProperty,
    light: "sun.png",
    dark:  "moon.png");
```

## Key points

- **No manual subscription required** — both approaches react to OS theme changes at runtime.
- **Works per-property**, so you can theme colors, images, fonts, etc. independently.
- To force a specific theme app-wide (overriding the device), set `Application.Current.UserAppTheme = AppTheme.Dark;`. Set it back to `AppTheme.Unspecified` to follow the system again.
